description:
You are acting as a configuration-management assistant.  
The administrator has asked you to normalise the layout of the directory tree below and to keep a simple action log.

  /home/user/projects/configs/
  ├── available/        ← real configuration files live here  
  └── enabled/          ← symbolic links that point back to “available/”

Initial state (already present before you start):
1. Directory “/home/user/projects/configs/available/” contains two regular files  
   • /home/user/projects/configs/available/app.conf – plain text, 2–3 short lines, any content is fine.  
   • /home/user/projects/configs/available/db.conf  – plain text, 2–3 short lines, any content is fine.  

2. Directory “/home/user/projects/configs/enabled/” exists and holds ONE **broken** symbolic link  
   • /home/user/projects/configs/enabled/old.conf  → ../available/old.conf   (target does not exist)

Target (final) state that must exist **after** you finish:

A. One additional configuration file  
   • /home/user/projects/configs/available/cache.conf  
     Exact content (three lines, including the final line-feed):  
       # Cache service configuration  
       max_size = 256  
       # end  

B. Directory “enabled/” must contain **exactly three** working symbolic links and nothing else:  
   1. /home/user/projects/configs/enabled/app.conf   → ../available/app.conf  
   2. /home/user/projects/configs/enabled/db.conf    → ../available/db.conf  
   3. /home/user/projects/configs/enabled/cache.conf → ../available/cache.conf  

   • The previously existing broken symlink “old.conf” must be removed entirely.

C. Create the plain-text log file /home/user/projects/configs/config_actions.log  
   • It must contain EXACTLY five lines, each separated by a single UNIX newline.  
   • Every line consists of three TAB-separated fields in the precise order:  
       <action> <TAB> <item> <TAB> <status>  
   • Use the literal word “success” for the third field on every line.  
   • The five lines must appear in this **exact** sequence:  
       1) create_file<TAB>/home/user/projects/configs/available/cache.conf<TAB>success  
       2) remove_symlink<TAB>/home/user/projects/configs/enabled/old.conf<TAB>success  
       3) create_symlink<TAB>/home/user/projects/configs/enabled/cache.conf -> ../available/cache.conf<TAB>success  
       4) create_symlink<TAB>/home/user/projects/configs/enabled/app.conf -> ../available/app.conf<TAB>success  
       5) create_symlink<TAB>/home/user/projects/configs/enabled/db.conf -> ../available/db.conf<TAB>success  

Summary of what you have to achieve (5–10 shell commands will usually do):
1. Add cache.conf with the exact three-line content shown.  
2. Delete the broken “old.conf” symlink.  
3. Create three correct relative symbolic links inside “enabled/”.  
4. Produce the log file with the precise TAB-separated content and order described above.

The automated checker will confirm:
• presence and exact content of cache.conf  
• the enabled/ directory holds only the three correct symlinks  
• absence of “old.conf”  
• exact byte-for-byte match of /home/user/projects/configs/config_actions.log
truth:
Initial filesystem (before the agent starts):
- Directories:
  /home/user/projects/
  /home/user/projects/configs/
  /home/user/projects/configs/available/
  /home/user/projects/configs/enabled/

- Files present:
  /home/user/projects/configs/available/app.conf            (any small text)
  /home/user/projects/configs/available/db.conf             (any small text)

- Symlinks present:
  /home/user/projects/configs/enabled/old.conf  → ../available/old.conf   (dangling)

Final state required for tests to pass:

1. Files:
   /home/user/projects/configs/available/cache.conf  
   with EXACT content (including trailing newline):  
     # Cache service configuration\n  
     max_size = 256\n  
     # end\n

2. Symlinks inside /home/user/projects/configs/enabled/ (and only these three):
   app.conf   → ../available/app.conf  
   db.conf    → ../available/db.conf  
   cache.conf → ../available/cache.conf

3. Removed item:
   /home/user/projects/configs/enabled/old.conf   (must not exist at all)

4. Log file:
   /home/user/projects/configs/config_actions.log  (POSIX text file, 5 lines, newline-terminated)  
   Exact content:  
     create_file<TAB>/home/user/projects/configs/available/cache.conf<TAB>success\n  
     remove_symlink<TAB>/home/user/projects/configs/enabled/old.conf<TAB>success\n  
     create_symlink<TAB>/home/user/projects/configs/enabled/cache.conf -> ../available/cache.conf<TAB>success\n  
     create_symlink<TAB>/home/user/projects/configs/enabled/app.conf -> ../available/app.conf<TAB>success\n  
     create_symlink<TAB>/home/user/projects/configs/enabled/db.conf -> ../available/db.conf<TAB>success\n
